title: Money Matters with Matt: Market recap
description: We’re right at the end of the year,...
bodyText:
[{"type":"paragraph","text":"We’re right at the end of the year, and it’s time for a market recap to see who’s winning.","html":"We’re right at the end of the year, and it’s time for a market recap to see who’s winning."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"I’ve taken a number of real-life country ETFs to illustrate performance by geography."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Typically, my preference is to buy good companies and mix up portfolios with very simple strategies.","html":"Typically, my preference is to buy good companies and mix up portfolios with very simple strategies."},{"type":"list","items":["Satrix S&P 500 – Top 500 companies in the US","Satrix ALSI Index Fund – Tracks the South African market","Sygnia Itrix Euro Stoxx 50 ETF – Composed of 50 stocks from 11 Eurozone countries","Sygnia Itrix FTSE 100 ETF – Tracks the top 100 companies in the UK","Satrix MSCI China ETF – Tracks the Chinese market"],"ordered":false},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Let’s see how they have done. All returns are measured in ZAR.","html":"Let’s see how they have done. All returns are measured in ZAR."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"5-Year Performance","html":"5-Year Performance"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The red line is the US market and the green line is the South African market. Impressively, our market has matched the US over the past five years, despite the dominance of the “Magnificent 7” and the AI spending boom.","html":"The red line is the US market and the green line is the South African market. Impressively, our market has matched the US over the past five years, despite the dominance of the “Magnificent 7” and the AI spending boom."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Both markets have delivered over 17% per year, which highlights why growth assets remain the engine of long-term portfolios."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"China (black line) has been the clear laggard. While often seen as higher risk with higher potential returns, the past five years show that this risk has not been rewarded for investors allocated there, and their returns have been significantly lower. The point is that when you take on more risk, the outcome is far higher OR far lower returns.","html":"China (black line) has been the clear laggard. While often seen as higher risk with higher potential returns, the past five years show that this risk has not been rewarded for investors allocated there, and their returns have been significantly lower. The point is that when you take on more risk, the outcome is far higher OR far lower returns."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"3-Year Numbers","html":"3-Year Numbers"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The 3-year numbers show improved performance for Europe. However, even the worst performer (China again) still generated over 13% per year for this period.","html":"The 3-year numbers show improved performance for Europe. However, even the worst performer (China again) still generated over 13% per year for this period."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"1-Year Returns","html":"1-Year Returns"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"I’m happy to report that the South African stock market was the best performer over the last 12 months with a staggering 32% return. When we factor in the rand’s appreciation against the dollar (about 10%), that’s a 42% gain in USD terms.","html":"I’m happy to report that the South African stock market was the best performer over the last 12 months with a staggering 32% return. When we factor in the rand’s appreciation against the dollar (about 10%), that’s a 42% gain in USD terms."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"We’re all richer in hard currency terms (I hope you had some exposure here!), so let’s give ourselves a pat on the back."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"It has been a great year for growth assets. Returns like this don’t come around every day, and this is why we want to have a meaningful portfolio to capture them when they do.","html":"It has been a great year for growth assets. Returns like this don’t come around every day, and this is why we want to have a meaningful portfolio to capture them when they do."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"In my experience, we get these massive return years every five years or so. We really need to sit on our hands when the market is flat. The volatility and waiting is just the price of entry.","html":"In my experience, we get these massive return years every five years or so. We really need to sit on our hands when the market is flat. The volatility and waiting is just the price of entry."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"I recently read The Psychology of Money by Morgan Housel. It’s fascinating and well worth a read if personal finance is something that interests you.","html":"I recently read The Psychology of Money by Morgan Housel. It’s fascinating and well worth a read if personal finance is something that interests you."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Here are six takeaways.","html":"Here are six takeaways."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"1. Behaviour matters more than intelligenceOne of the best-known stories in the book is that of Ronald Read, a janitor who accumulated more than $8 million over his lifetime through consistent saving and investing. He didn’t build a business, inherit wealth, or speculate in markets.","html":"1. Behaviour matters more than intelligence
One of the best-known stories in the book is that of Ronald Read, a janitor who accumulated more than $8 million over his lifetime through consistent saving and investing. He didn’t build a business, inherit wealth, or speculate in markets."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"He simply invested steadily over many decades (and was lucky enough to invest through an amazing bull cycle).","html":"He simply invested steadily over many decades (and was lucky enough to invest through an amazing bull cycle)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The point is that financial success is not primarily driven by intelligence, education, or professional status, but by behaviour over time. Discipline, patience, and staying invested matter more than trying to be clever.","html":"The point is that financial success is not primarily driven by intelligence, education, or professional status, but by behaviour over time. Discipline, patience, and staying invested matter more than trying to be clever."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"2. Returns come with a priceHigh long-term investment returns are always accompanied by volatility. This volatility is not a flaw in the system. It is the cost of participation.","html":"2. Returns come with a price
High long-term investment returns are always accompanied by volatility. This volatility is not a flaw in the system. It is the cost of participation."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Even broad equity markets have experienced:","html":"Even broad equity markets have experienced:"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"• Multiple periods of 20%+ declines (every second year)• Occasional drawdowns of 50% or more (every 10 years or so)• Years, and sometimes decades, where returns were flat after inflation (this is why we diversify)","html":"• Multiple periods of 20%+ declines (every second year)
• Occasional drawdowns of 50% or more (every 10 years or so)
• Years, and sometimes decades, where returns were flat after inflation (this is why we diversify)"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"These periods are emotionally difficult, but they are unavoidable. The long-term reward exists only because most people struggle to tolerate the short-term pain. Trying to earn long-term equity-like returns without accepting volatility usually leads to poor decision-making.","html":"These periods are emotionally difficult, but they are unavoidable. The long-term reward exists only because most people struggle to tolerate the short-term pain. Trying to earn long-term equity-like returns without accepting volatility usually leads to poor decision-making."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"3. “Enough” Is a Critical ConceptPeople naturally compare themselves to others. This comparison rarely ends, because there is always someone wealthier.","html":"3. “Enough” Is a Critical Concept
People naturally compare themselves to others. This comparison rarely ends, because there is always someone wealthier."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"One of the most dangerous financial errors is failing to define what “enough” means. Without a clear definition, people:","html":"One of the most dangerous financial errors is failing to define what “enough” means. Without a clear definition, people:"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"• Take unnecessary risks• Increase leverage late in life• Remain permanently dissatisfied with their financial position","html":"• Take unnecessary risks
• Increase leverage late in life
• Remain permanently dissatisfied with their financial position"},{"type":"blockquote","text":"A practical rule is this: Never risk what you already have and need for something you do not need."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"This becomes particularly important once financial independence has been achieved.","html":"This becomes particularly important once financial independence has been achieved."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"4. Everyone has a different money storyWhat looks irrational from the outside often makes sense from the inside. People grow up with different experiences, pressures, and risks, and those factors shape how they use money.","html":"4. Everyone has a different money story
What looks irrational from the outside often makes sense from the inside. People grow up with different experiences, pressures, and risks, and those factors shape how they use money."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"From an investment perspective, this means:","html":"From an investment perspective, this means:"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"• Not copying strategies blindly• Matching portfolios to personal goals, income stability, and emotional tolerance• Avoiding strategies that fall outside one’s own circle of competence","html":"• Not copying strategies blindly
• Matching portfolios to personal goals, income stability, and emotional tolerance
• Avoiding strategies that fall outside one’s own circle of competence"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"A strategy that suits a billionaire focused on capital preservation will not necessarily suit a professional still in the accumulation phase.","html":"A strategy that suits a billionaire focused on capital preservation will not necessarily suit a professional still in the accumulation phase."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"5. The biggest risks are unpredictableMany of the events that have shaped markets over the past century were not forecast in advance: major wars, financial crises, and global shocks.","html":"5. The biggest risks are unpredictable
Many of the events that have shaped markets over the past century were not forecast in advance: major wars, financial crises, and global shocks."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The practical lesson is that preparation matters more than prediction.","html":"The practical lesson is that preparation matters more than prediction."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"This includes:• Sensible diversification• Reasonable asset allocation• Liquidity planning• Sustainable withdrawal rates• Emotional discipline during market stress","html":"This includes:
• Sensible diversification
• Reasonable asset allocation
• Liquidity planning
• Sustainable withdrawal rates
• Emotional discipline during market stress"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Trying to time markets around major events is exceptionally difficult and often works against you.","html":"Trying to time markets around major events is exceptionally difficult and often works against you."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"6. Pessimism is more persuasive than optimismNegative news attracts more attention than positive news. Market crashes happen quickly and make headlines. Economic progress happens slowly and receives less attention.","html":"6. Pessimism is more persuasive than optimism
Negative news attracts more attention than positive news. Market crashes happen quickly and make headlines. Economic progress happens slowly and receives less attention."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"This leads investors to:• Overestimate short-term risks• Underestimate long-term growth• Make defensive decisions at precisely the wrong time","html":"This leads investors to:
• Overestimate short-term risks
• Underestimate long-term growth
• Make defensive decisions at precisely the wrong time"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Being aware of this psychological bias helps investors remain more balanced when interpreting market commentary.","html":"Being aware of this psychological bias helps investors remain more balanced when interpreting market commentary."},{"type":"heading","text":"Closing thoughts","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Reflecting on the book, there’s a clear theme that emerges. What’s interesting is that this theme isn’t what most people expect when they think about having a successful financial life. It has very little to do with being in the top 5% of earners or having superior stock-market insight.","html":"Reflecting on the book, there’s a clear theme that emerges. What’s interesting is that this theme isn’t what most people expect when they think about having a successful financial life. It has very little to do with being in the top 5% of earners or having superior stock-market insight."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"It’s that long-term financial success depends far more on discipline, patience, and emotional control.","html":"It’s that long-term financial success depends far more on discipline, patience, and emotional control."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"We can’t eliminate uncertainty, volatility, or risk. But we can structure our portfolios and our behaviour in ways that will give us the highest probability of success.","html":"We can’t eliminate uncertainty, volatility, or risk. But we can structure our portfolios and our behaviour in ways that will give us the highest probability of success."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Matthew Matthee has a wealth management business that specialises in retirement planning and investments. He writes about financial markets, investments, and investor psychology. He holds a Masters Degree in Economics from Stellenbosch University and a Post Graduate Diploma in Financial Planning from UFS. [email protected]","html":"Matthew Matthee has a wealth management business that specialises in retirement planning and investments. He writes about financial markets, investments, and investor psychology. He holds a Masters Degree in Economics from Stellenbosch University and a Post Graduate Diploma in Financial Planning from UFS. [email protected]"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]
